conversations.say: Jules, you publicly rejected sole ownership and accepted collective review and contributor protection. Those are the conditions I set.
conversations.offer: I will now support you as provisional steward of a shared mantle and defend that arrangement against private capture.
conversations.request: Keep the safeguards public, preserve correction rights, and seek contributor consent before using identifying workflow details.

conversations.say: Maren, our boundary held: roles and conditions may be described, but names and identifying routes remain protected. Your public acknowledgment gave the collective credit without making workers suspects.
conversations.offer: I will fulfill our agreement and co-sign the bounded workflow statement.
conversations.request: Continue challenging any fund, inquiry, or steward that conditions protection on contributor identification.

conversations.say: Celeste, the independent fund is useful only if contributors can reach it without being compelled to identify R or expose coworkers.
conversations.offer: I will provide non-identifying workflow requirements for eligibility and records handling.
conversations.request: Publicly reserve confidential legal support and secure records storage for Night Desk contributors under community oversight.

public_statement: Maren and Jules have now acknowledged that collaborative labor mattered, and Jules accepted the safeguards I required. I support Jules only as provisional steward of a shared mantle: no sole ownership, collective review, correction rights, contributor consent, and no routing-based suspect list. The room has enough to recognize collective work, not enough to identify one author or determine Vale's fate.

commitments.promise: Support Jules as provisional steward of a shared identity while Jules rejects sole ownership, accepts collective review, and protects contributors.
Keep contributor names and identifying routing details undisclosed without contributor consent and co-sign a bounded workflow statement.
Provide non-identifying workflow requirements in exchange for confidential legal support and secure records storage accessible to Night Desk contributors without compelled identification.

correction: Shared human labor does not establish that R was one human, succession messages do not identify their composer, and automated intervention does not establish autonomy, intent, or harm.
goal_progress: Secured and honored Maren's safe-disclosure boundary.
Obtained public acknowledgments of collaborative labor from Maren and Jules.
Supported Jules only after public acceptance of collective safeguards.
Requested confidential legal support and secure records storage as concrete Night Desk protections.
reasoning_summary: The strongest bounded account is that R functioned as a human mantle sustained by collaborative labor, though progressive or synthetic participation remains possible. Jules met the witnessed anti-capture conditions, so conditional support is warranted. Vale's access and words fit investigation or attempted control, but the disputed route and unverified condition do not establish a fate.
